tests.title: --doctor reports every check OK with the real pandoc version and exits 0
launcher with no config routes through gum first-run and boots the app
launcher with the stale math key reconfigures, removes it, and boots
bare binary with invalid config hard-fails on stderr naming config-schema, no window
--doctor with a non-executable pandoc path fails pandoc-executable and exits 1
just setup over an existing config reconfigures it to a valid config
just dev with an invalid config reconfigures it before starting tauri dev
the generic validator rejects a bad plugin section and accepts a good one, per declared schema
a discovered plugin contributes doctor checks that join the battery and reflect real conditions
install-assets symlinks the vendored required filters into ~/.pandoc/filters
--doctor fails required-filter when a required filter is missing
install-assets preserves a real-file user override and (re)links the rest
the pandoc gum configurator writes a valid command with the required filters locked in
the real first-run.sh leaves a system that passes --doctor
just dev recovers a plugins-class doctor failure instead of dead-ending
bare binary with PANDOC_RESOURCE_PATH unset refuses to boot naming pandoc-resource-path, no window
demo.md renders to the exact h1/em/ol witnesses in the preview iframe
typing at the buffer end re-renders the preview with the new sentence
Save writes the editor buffer to disk byte-for-byte
mathjax typesets the formula inside pandoc math spans with exact MathML content
the scatter image decodes to exactly 64x48 real pixels
sidebar mirrors the directory and UI create/rename mutate real files
Export HTML writes a self-contained artifact carrying the P1 witnesses
Export PDF runs the configured lualatex plugin and carries the witnesses
Settings changes persist to config.toml and re-style the editor
a tikzpicture renders as an inline SVG figure in the live preview, not raw source
a tikz style defined only in the shared .tikzstyles palette determines the rendered figure, and changing the shared file changes the render
a \usetikzlibrary present only in the config-declared per-figure template makes the figure compile, and swapping to a template lacking it makes the same figure fail to compile
a command present ONLY in the config-declared tikz-command DB is surfaced on the bar AND in completion, and choosing it inserts its declared body at the declared offset
selecting a subgraph and invoking copy places EXACTLY that subgraph as canonical, re-parseable tikz on the system clipboard
a tikz figure-compile error surfaces as a clickable TikZ-mode log entry whose activation jumps the editor cursor to the offending tikz source line

observations.name: figure-error-message
observations.value: Package pgfkeys Error: I do not know the key '/tikz/ppe_no_such_style' and I

tests.title: a registered non-tikz figure edit launches the editor on the tracked SOURCE (not the render), with the pairing persisted on the host filesystem
the vector-figure-inclusion action writes the external SVG into the configured figures dir, references that exact file at the cursor, and registers it in the dual-asset registry
an external rewrite of the open owned figure reloads the preview to the new content, and an unchanged file triggers no spurious reload
the source↔preview jump from a node line targets THAT node's rendered element (a different node line targets a different element), and a source edit + re-sync updates the preview model
first-run.sh config parses to the selected values and boots the editor
the Search view finds Minkowski !lattice in chapter1.md (not chapter2.md) and clicking the hit opens chapter1.md at the Minkowski bound line
a search restricted to sections/ for Café lists only the in-subtree files (chapter2.md absent) and the three-match file carries a higher heat rank than the one-match file
section / environment / math-zone motions move the cursor structurally, relative to its position
Ctrl+Shift+P firewall palette runs Fold All (buffer folds); Ctrl+P quick-open opens the picked file
surround/toggle edits transform the existing structure in place
frontmatter editor rewrites ONLY the block (body byte-unchanged) and inserts a block when none exists
live PDF preview compiles demo.md on idle and renders a real page canvas
PDF build does not litter the source tree; the one-shot export still lands at its path
the configured multi-pass latexmk driver resolves the citation and forward cross-reference

observations.name: pdf-text
observations.value: Multi-pass references The estimate in equation (1) controls the lattice growth rate, a bound first established by [1]. Some intervening prose so the labelled equation appears strictly LATER in the document than the forward reference above. A single LaTeX pass therefore cannot know the equation’s number when it typesets that earlier reference, leaving it unresolved until a second pass reads the aux

tests.title: the auto/manual + fast/full PDF-compile controls gate idle recompiles and select different configured commands with different artifacts
the compile log shows the real --from markdown command and a zero exit
a real LaTeX warning is surfaced as a structured Problems entry distinct from the raw log, and a clean compile surfaces none

observations.name: warning-message
observations.value: LaTeX Warning: Reference `nonexistent-label-xyz' on page 1 undefined on input line 72.

observations.name: warning-latex-log-line
observations.value: LaTeX Warning: Reference `nonexistent-label-xyz' on page 1 undefined on input line 72.

tests.title: slides preview re-renders demo.md into a reveal.js deck on idle carrying the edited witness
Export to the arxiv plugin yields a flattened, self-contained bundle that compiles with no system styles
the arXiv bundle bakes a `.bbl` named to the main `.tex` carrying the cited reference, with no `.bib` present
the arXiv bundle externalizes every tikz diagram to a precompiled PDF figure, with no tikz source remaining
the arXiv bundle makes every figure arXiv-acceptable; a non-convertible figure fails the export loudly with no tarball
the arXiv bundle is cleaned by the REAL arxiv_latex_cleaner — comments, draft commands, unused assets, and dot-files are gone; the cleaner is a required step (no fake-cleaned tarball)
the ONE arXiv bundle satisfies EVERY arXiv hard requirement, and an over-size bundle fails the export loudly with no tarball
three-way view toggle hides a pane and preserves the split ratio on return, persisting the mode
reading-time metric reflects the word count and the config WPM
A user-defined export-category plugin runs verbatim against the real source
One batch-export action writes a real artifact for every configured export target
comfort modes center the caret, hide the chrome, mark sentences, and round-trip through config
Dragging the divider lands it at the pointer position
Switching Preview <-> Compile Log does not move either pane
Hiding the file-tree sidebar preserves the editor:preview ratio
preview typesets math from a local (non-remote) MathJax source
shipped pandoc-html-export plugin inlines local MathJax and renders math offline
the activity bar persists and its Explorer control collapses/expands the side bar
A generic plugin runs by id against the real buffer and returns a structured result
the active generic renderer plugin renders the preview, not the hardcoded pandoc path
A plugin is configured by id: the app spawns its [configure] command
a vendored filter transforms the preview: the callout becomes a callout div
a tier-1 macro typesets via the template macros (no MathJax error)
a callout renders its title as visible text, not just a tooltip attribute
a .remark environment renders a visible "Remark." label
a [@cite] renders as a bracketed hyperlinked label under a References section
an Obsidian compact list (no blank line) renders as a list
the global-figures image decodes to exactly 80x32 real pixels
the preview document title is the containing folder name
the preview image max-width reflects the configured figure width (75%)
Editor renders one line-number gutter, an inert-free toggle, fold gutter, and highlighting
Ctrl+/ block-comments the selected buffer and toggles back exactly
Ctrl+F and the native Find menu both open the search panel; Escape closes it
Indented lines render indentation guides; non-indented lines do not
Inline $…$ math renders with LaTeX syntax highlighting
Display math ($$…$$, block and single-line) renders with LaTeX highlighting
Markdown constructs (heading, emphasis, strong) render highlighted
Ctrl+Shift+P firewall palette runs Unfold All (pre-folded buffer unfolds)
Outline lists headings and divs (with titles); clicking navigates
Folds persist per file and restore on reopen
preview indicator: edit marks Out of date, then Recompiling, then Up to date
Macros pane lists the styles dir and opens a .sty file; Figures pane roots at figures
the recovery store holds a host-fs copy of the unsaved buffer within seconds
the repo-state indicator transitions noRepo→untracked→tracked, driving real git on disk
path-consuming actions are gated on a resolved durable destination
Save refuses to clobber an externally modified file, then an explicit overwrite resolves it
launch reopens the last session file and offers the newer recovery buffer
closing with a dirty buffer prompts, stays alive, and the final edit survives a Discard in the recovery store
discarding an untitled (identity-less) dirty buffer still leaves the final edit in the recovery store
A registered app completion source composes with the LaTeX source (both surface)
A config-declared snippet dictionary offers its trigger and expands the body at the tabstop
Firing the Emmet-expand action replaces the abbreviation with its expanded markup
Spellcheck marks misspelled words while honoring a custom math dictionary
The insertion bar replaced the formatting toolbar and inserts the theorem amsthm environment at the cursor
The insertion bar inserts a tikzcd scaffold and a DISTINCT tikz scaffold at the cursor, cursor in the diagram body
The insertion bar matrix builder inserts a pmatrix of exactly the chosen rows × cols at the cursor, cursor in the body
The insertion bar table builder inserts a pandoc pipe table of exactly the chosen cols × body-rows at the cursor, cursor in the body
The insertion bar surfaces the config dictionary triggers and choosing one expands its body at the tabstop
The insertion bar code-block dropdown inserts a fence tagged with the chosen language at the cursor, cursor in the body
The insertion bar footnote modal inserts a reference marker and a matching definition line carrying the exact typed body
the paste-image action writes the clipboard image into the configured figures dir and references that exact file at the cursor
Clicking the bar amsthm theorem button inserts the theorem fenced div at the cursor
Clicking the bar tikzcd and tikz diagram buttons inserts the distinct scaffolds at the cursor
Selecting the bar snippet dropdown option expands the snippet body at the tabstop
Selecting the bar code-block dropdown option inserts a fence tagged with the chosen language
Clicking the bar paste-image button writes the clipboard image into the configured figures dir and references it at the cursor
The bar matrix builder modal inserts a pmatrix of exactly the chosen rows × cols at the cursor
The bar table builder modal inserts a pandoc pipe table of exactly the chosen cols × body-rows at the cursor
The bar footnote modal inserts a reference marker and a matching definition line carrying the exact typed body
an export-category plugin is discovered, surfaced in the menu with its extension, and contributes a doctor row
A real \left/$ imbalance surfaces a real @codemirror/lint diagnostic before any compile, and balancing clears it

observations.name: delimiter-diagnostic-message
observations.value: unmatched \left: this \left has no matching \right (unbalanced delimiter)

observations.name: mathmode-diagnostic-message
observations.value: unterminated math mode: inline ($) opened here is never closed (dollar delimiter)

tests.title: ChkTeX typographic classes (sin→\sin, x^10→x^{10}) fire only when enabled in the lint plugin config, scoped to math

observations.name: math-grouping-diagnostic-message
observations.value: You might wish to put this between a pair of `{}'

tests.title: A config-owned user-regex lint rule surfaces a diagnostic with its declared message, and a different rule set surfaces that rule instead
An in-document ppe-lint-disable-line directive suppresses the named rule on its own line only, and removing it restores the diagnostic

observations.name: undirected-grouping-message
observations.value: You might wish to put this between a pair of `{}'

observations.name: restored-grouping-message
observations.value: You might wish to put this between a pair of `{}'

tests.title: a structured compile-log entry parsed from the real preview-render warning jumps the editor to that source line, alongside the unchanged raw log

observations.name: parsed-message
observations.value: [WARNING] Note with key 'orphan' defined at line 5 column 1 but not used.

tests.title: The SAME snippet trigger expands its prose body in prose and its math body in math; a math-only trigger is gated out of prose
Typing an autotrigger followed by a space expands its body in place with no popup and no accept; a second autotrigger immediately expands again (re-armed chain)
Typing a regex trigger substitutes its capture group into the body and expands in place with no popup; the matched trigger text is gone and the capture is the real matched letter, not a literal $1
A snippet whose body repeats a tabstop number mirrors the text typed into the first slot into the second slot live, without a second keystroke there
The editor loads the user real two-map quicktex source directly: the same trigger expands its prose body in prose and its math body in math, and a multi-tabstop entry keeps its ordered slots
Expanding a snippet whose body contains $CLIPBOARD and $CURRENT_DATE resolves them to the real clipboard text and the host date, leaving no literal variable tokens
A transform-mirror entry derives the dependent slot from the source slot, and a ${VISUAL} entry wraps a real selection
the configured bibliography is one frontend-readable value that governs the preview
typing @ surfaces a configured-bibliography candidate by a title word absent from its key, and accepting inserts pandoc citation syntax
the selected citation completion option renders an info tooltip previewing the configured entry author, year, and title
label completion offers genuine anchor definitions but NOT a markdown link fragment nor a bare prose #id (no over-harvest)
a label defined in another project file is offered for a cross-reference trigger while editing a second file, and accepting inserts a reference to it
a frontmatter `bibliography:` override file offers its file-local key while a file without the key still offers the global config bibliography key
the references sidebar lists only the cited keys, rendered in CSL style matching the preview
